request.question: Girl Alone was sponsored by an American multinational food manufacturing company that produces cereal and convenience foods, and is headquartered where?

title: La Loma Foods
Tee Yih Jia
Ken's Foods
Kellogg's
GoodMark Foods
PepsiCo
Convenience food
Bright Food
Patties Foods
Girl Alone
sentences: La Loma Foods, formerly named Loma Linda Food Company and Loma Linda Foods, and with products presently branded under the name Loma Linda and Loma, is a former food manufacturing company that produced vegetarian and vegan foods.
It is presently an active brand of vegetarian and vegan food products produced and purveyed by the Atlantic Natural Foods Company of Nashville, North Carolina.
Loma Linda Foods began operations in 1905 under the name The Sanitarium Food Company and was owned by the Seventh-day Adventist Church until 1990.
Tee Yih Jia Food Manufacturing Pte Ltd (simplified Chinese: 第一家), is a food manufacturing company headquartered in Singapore.

Tee Yih Jia is the world leading manufacturer of spring roll pastry (also known as "popiah" in local context) that also manufactures a wide range of ready-to-eat Asian convenience foods such as roti paratha, crepes, cocktail prawn rolls, glutinous rice balls, spring rolls and samosas.

Apart from pastry-based food, Tee Yih Jia manufactures other frozen convenience food products such as Dim Sum, Ramen, Oriental Buns (or mantou), and Asian Pizzas with flavours like Peking duck and satay chicken.
Tee Yih Jia's "Spring Home" brand products are manufactured in Singapore as well as in several regional facilities.
Tee Yih Jia has a 400000 sqft factory in Senoko (Singapore) and a Johor (Malaysia) factory of 200000 sqft , as well as manufacturing plants in the USA and China.
Ken's Foods is a privately held food manufacturing company located in Marlborough, Massachusetts.
Ken's primary products are salad dressings, sauces, and marinades.
The company's commercial food manufacturing divisions produce products both for retail sale and food service, including contract manufacturing for companies such as Newman's Own.
The Kellogg Company (also Kellogg's, Kellogg, and Kellogg's of Battle Creek) is an American multinational food manufacturing company headquartered in Battle Creek, Michigan, United States.
Kellogg's produces cereal and convenience foods, including cookies, crackers, toaster pastries, cereal bars, fruit-flavored snacks, frozen waffles, and vegetarian foods.

The company's brands include Froot Loops, Apple Jacks, Corn Flakes, Frosted Flakes, Rice Krispies, Special K, Cocoa Krispies, Keebler, Pringles, Pop-Tarts, Kashi, Cheez-It, Eggo, Nutri-Grain, Morningstar Farms, and many more.
Kellogg's stated purpose is "Nourishing families so they can flourish and thrive."
Goodmark Foods, Inc. is an American food manufacturing company, based in Raleigh, North Carolina.
It produces "meat snacks, packaged meats, and extruded grain snacks," especially Slim Jim jerky meat snacks.
It is owned by ConAgra.
PepsiCo, Inc. is an American multinational food, snack, and beverage corporation headquartered in Purchase, New York.
PepsiCo has interests in the manufacturing, marketing, and distribution of grain-based snack foods, beverages, and other products.
PepsiCo was formed in 1965 with the merger of the Pepsi-Cola Company and Frito-Lay, Inc.

PepsiCo has since expanded from its namesake product Pepsi to a broader range of food and beverage brands, the largest of which included an acquisition of Tropicana Products in 1998 and the Quaker Oats Company in 2001, which added the Gatorade brand to its portfolio.
Convenience food, or tertiary processed food, is food that is commercially prepared (often through processing) to optimise ease of consumption.
Such food is usually ready to eat without further preparation.
It may also be easily portable, have a long shelf life, or offer a combination of such convenient traits.
Although restaurant meals meet this definition, the term is seldom applied to them.
Convenience foods include ready-to-eat dry products, frozen foods such as TV dinners, shelf-stable foods, prepared mixes such as cake mix, and snack foods.
Bright Food (officially Bright Food (Group) Co., Ltd.) is a multinational food and beverages manufacturing company headquartered in Shanghai, China.
It is the second-largest China-based food manufacturing company measured by 2011 revenues.
The company is wholly owned by the Shanghai Municipal Government via Shanghai Municipal Investment Group and another sister company.
Patties Foods, is an Australian food manufacturing company that produces meat pies, baked goods, frozen fruits, and pre-made desserts.
Patties Foods is represented in the Australian market by the Four'N Twenty, Patties, Herbert Adams, Creative Gourmet, Nanna's, Chefs Pride and Snowy River brands.
Patties is the largest Meat pie producing company in Australia, and the world.
They are the producers of several well-known Meat pie's, including the Patties, Snowy River pie, Herbert Adams pie, and as of 2003, Four'N Twenty pies.
Patties Foods is headquartered in Bairnsdale, Victoria.
Girl Alone was an American radio soap opera broadcast on NBC from 1935 to 1941.
Sponsored by Kellogg's and Quaker Oats, the series was scripted by Fayette Krum.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Girl Alone was sponsored by an American multinational food manufacturing company that produces cereal and convenience foods, and is headquartered where?
hop1_docs: Kellogg's | The Kellogg Company (also Kellogg's, Kellogg, and Kellogg's of Battle Creek) is an American multinational food manufacturing company headquartered in Battle Creek, Michigan, United States.  Kellogg's produces cereal and convenience foods, including cookies, crackers, toaster pastries, cereal bars, fruit-flavored snacks, frozen waffles, and vegetarian foods.  The company's brands include Froot Loops, Apple Jacks, Corn Flakes, Frosted Flakes, Rice Krispies, Special K, Cocoa Krispies, Keebler, Pringles, Pop-Tarts, Kashi, Cheez-It, Eggo, Nutri-Grain, Morningstar Farms, and many more.  Kellogg's stated purpose is "Nourishing families so they can flourish and thrive."

Bright Food | Bright Food (officially Bright Food (Group) Co., Ltd.) is a multinational food and beverages manufacturing company headquartered in Shanghai, China.  It is the second-largest China-based food manufacturing company measured by 2011 revenues.  The company is wholly owned by the Shanghai Municipal Government via Shanghai Municipal Investment Group and another sister company.

Flavour and Life | Flavour and Life (FAL) Food and Beverages is a multinational food and beverage company headquartered in Sydney, Australia, with interests in the manufacturing, producing, marketing, and distribution of health-based foods, beverages, cosmetics, and other products.  It is best known for its Coco Joy line of coconut-based foods and beverages.
Siniora (company) | Siniora Food and Manufacturing plc.  Is a multinational food industry company that produces meat products in Palestine, Jordan, and Saudi Arabia.

Convenient Food Mart | Convenient Food Mart (CFM) is a chain of convenience stores in the United States.  The private company's headquarters are located in Mentor, Ohio, and there are currently approximately 325 stores located in the US.  Convenient Food Mart operates on the franchise system.

Bell Food Group | The Bell Food Group is the leading meat processor and manufacturer of convenience products in Switzerland.  The company was founded in 1869 by Samuel Bell in Basel, where its headquarters is located up to now.  Its range of products includes meat, poultry, charcuterie, seafood and convenience products.  Brands include Bell, Abraham, ZIMBO, Môssieur Polette and Hilcona.  Some 8,000 employees generated consolidated sales of CHF 2.6 billion in the fiscal year 2014.  Bell is listed on the Swiss stock exchange.  The majority shareholder of Bell is Coop, one of Switzerland's largest retail and wholesale companies.

McKee Foods | McKee Foods Corporation is a privately held and family owned American snack food and granola manufacturer headquartered in Collegedale, Tennessee.  The corporation is the maker of the Little Debbie Snacks, Sunbelt Bakery granola and cereal, Heartland Brands, and Drake's Cakes.  McKee Foods has its own fleet of trucks to distribute its products.
response.summary_1: Passage [1] identifies The Kellogg Company (Kellogg's) as "an American multinational food manufacturing company" that "produces cereal and convenience foods" and states it "is headquartered in Battle Creek, Michigan, United States." The passages do NOT explicitly state that Kellogg's sponsored Girl Alone—next hop: retrieve a source on the show "Girl Alone" (or contemporaneous sponsorship records) to confirm that Kellogg's is the sponsor (or to identify the actual sponsoring company). Use the quoted anchors "produces cereal and convenience foods" and "is headquartered in Battle Creek, Michigan, United States" when matching the sponsor to Kellogg's.
response.hop2_query: "Girl Alone" sponsor "Kellogg Company" Battle Creek Michigan
hop2_docs: Kellogg's | The Kellogg Company (also Kellogg's, Kellogg, and Kellogg's of Battle Creek) is an American multinational food manufacturing company headquartered in Battle Creek, Michigan, United States.  Kellogg's produces cereal and convenience foods, including cookies, crackers, toaster pastries, cereal bars, fruit-flavored snacks, frozen waffles, and vegetarian foods.  The company's brands include Froot Loops, Apple Jacks, Corn Flakes, Frosted Flakes, Rice Krispies, Special K, Cocoa Krispies, Keebler, Pringles, Pop-Tarts, Kashi, Cheez-It, Eggo, Nutri-Grain, Morningstar Farms, and many more.  Kellogg's stated purpose is "Nourishing families so they can flourish and thrive."
Girl Alone | Girl Alone was an American radio soap opera broadcast on NBC from 1935 to 1941.  Sponsored by Kellogg's and Quaker Oats, the series was scripted by Fayette Krum.

Battle Creek Crunch | The Battle Creek Crunch were a professional indoor football team based in Battle Creek, Michigan.  The team was a charter member of the Great Lakes Indoor Football League joining the league in 2006 as an expansion team.  The Crunch were the first professional indoor football team to be based in Battle Creek.  The Crunch were owned by Mike Powell.  They played their home games at the Kellogg Arena in Battle Creek, Michigan.

Kellogg Community College | Kellogg Community College (KCC) is a public college founded in 1956 that provides academic, occupational, general and lifelong learning opportunities on its main campus in Battle Creek, Michigan, online and at other campuses in Southwest Michigan.  Satellite campuses include the Eastern Academic Center, located in Albion; the Fehsenfeld Center, located in Hastings; the Grahl Center, located in Coldwater; and the Regional Manufacturing Technology Center, located in Battle Creek.

Corn flakes | Corn flakes, or cornflakes, are a popular breakfast cereal made by toasting flakes of corn.  The cereal was first created by John Harvey Kellogg in 1894 as a food that he thought would be healthy for the patients of the Battle Creek Sanitarium in Michigan where he was superintendent.  The breakfast cereal proved popular among the patients and the Kellogg Company (Kellogg's) was set up to produce corn flakes for the wider public.  A patent for the process was granted in 1896.

John Harvey Kellogg | John Harvey Kellogg, M.D. (February 26, 1852 – December 14, 1943) was an American medical doctor, nutritionist, inventor, health activist, and businessman.  He was the director of the Battle Creek Sanitarium in Battle Creek, Michigan.  The sanitarium was founded by members of the Seventh-Day Adventist Church.  It combined aspects of a European spa, a hydrotherapy institution, a hospital and a high-class hotel.  Kellogg treated both the rich and famous and the poor who could not go to other hospitals.

Kellogg's Cereal City USA | Kellogg's Cereal City USA was a tourist attraction in the downtown area of the city of Battle Creek, Michigan (itself nicknamed "Cereal City"), open to visitors between 1998 and 2007.  The attraction aimed to tell visitors the story of the Kellogg's brand, its products and contribution to the breakfast cereal industry in particular.
response.summary_2: Girl Alone was sponsored by Kellogg's; The Kellogg Company is headquartered in Battle Creek, Michigan, United States.
